key_insight: PA-SciML makes agentic SciML discovery verification-first: candidates are accepted only after passing explicit, machine-checkable physics audits, not just low error.
engineering_relevance: For constitutive modeling and solid mechanics, this helps ensure surrogates respect boundary conditions, scaling, superposition, and causality before they are trusted in design or simulation workflows.
method: Fix the scoring evaluator before search.
Derive reviewable physics requirements and audit each candidate’s predicted fields.
Search prescribed input/load-history spans for worst-case violations; optionally add advisory numerical probes and isolate one modeling change at a time.
evidence_signal: In the reported elasticity and elastodynamics examples, the selected surrogate had lower validation error than an error-only baseline in the static case, while the transient case showed an error-only baseline with similar mean error failing a causality check.
watch_item: Physics audits are only as strong as the chosen checks and input-span coverage, so untested regimes or incomplete requirements can still let nonphysical surrogates pass.
paper_title: Physics-Audited Agentic Discovery in Scientific Machine Learning
